name: Prudens Purple
tagline: Old-fashioned beefsteak with rich, sweet flavour
description: A pre-1900 Amish heirloom producing large pink-purple beefsteak tomatoes. Excellent flavour balance of sweet and acid.
regionNotes: Thrives in temperate climates with warm summers. UK growers should start indoors 6-8 weeks before last frost.

name: Arkansas Traveler
tagline: Heat-tolerant Southern classic, crack-resistant
description: Heat and humidity tolerant heirloom from the American South. Produces medium-sized pink-red fruit even in hot weather.
regionNotes: Best suited to warm temperate and subtropical climates.

name: Chico
tagline: Compact paste tomato, perfect for sauces
description: Determinate plum-shaped paste tomato with thick walls and few seeds. Ideal for batch processing.
regionNotes: Adapts to most climates. UK growers benefit from a sheltered south-facing position.

name: Better Boy
tagline: Disease-resistant all-rounder, reliable harvest
description: One of the most popular hybrids worldwide. Strong disease resistance, produces medium-large slicer tomatoes consistently.
regionNotes: Adaptable to most climates. Especially recommended for new growers in any zone.

name: Florida 91
tagline: Heat-set hybrid, fruits in extreme heat
description: Specifically bred to set fruit in high temperatures where other varieties fail. Determinate, compact, strong disease resistance.
regionNotes: Best for hot climates. Not recommended for cool UK summers.

name: Jetsetter
tagline: Early-ripening, perfect for cool climates
description: Fast-maturing hybrid producing medium-large red slicers in just 64 days. Excellent disease resistance.
regionNotes: Ideal for short-season climates: UK, northern Europe, Canada.

name: Lucid Gem
tagline: Striking yellow-red bicolour heirloom
description: Visually stunning variety with golden yellow skin marbled with red. Sweet, fruity flavour.
regionNotes: Performs well in temperate climates with a long growing season.

name: Cherokee Carbon
tagline: Dark, smoky-flavoured heirloom cross
description: Cross between Cherokee Purple and Carbon. Dark purple-black beefsteaks with intense, complex flavour.
regionNotes: Great match for temperate climates. UK growers: provide shelter or greenhouse.

name: Chef's Choice Orange
tagline: All-America Selections winner, fruity flavour
description: Award-winning hybrid producing large, deep-orange beefsteaks with a sweet, fruity flavour.
regionNotes: Adapts well to most temperate climates. Reliable for UK gardeners.

name: Super Fantastic
tagline: Reliable workhorse hybrid, crack-resistant
description: Heavy-yielding indeterminate hybrid producing medium-large red globes. Strong disease resistance.
regionNotes: Adapts to most climates. Reliable for variable weather conditions.

name: Indian Stripe
tagline: Cherokee Purple cousin with green shoulders
description: Heirloom from the Cherokee tribe, similar to Cherokee Purple. Rich, complex flavour and meaty texture.
regionNotes: Best in warm temperate climates. Greenhouse cultivation recommended in cooler regions.

name: La Roma
tagline: Improved Roma paste, large and meaty
description: Improved Roma-type with larger, meatier fruit than traditional Roma. Determinate growth, excellent for sauces.
regionNotes: Versatile across climates. Mediterranean and temperate zones produce excellent yields.

name: Juan Flamme
tagline: French heirloom, apricot-orange salad tomato
description: French heirloom producing 80g apricot-orange round tomatoes. Bright, fruity, tangy flavour.
regionNotes: Excellent UK and French performer. One of the best for temperate maritime climates.

name: Brandy Boy
tagline: Brandywine flavour with hybrid disease resistance
description: Cross designed to capture Brandywine flavour with modern disease resistance. Large pink-red beefsteaks.
regionNotes: Versatile in temperate climates. Reliable for UK gardeners wanting beefsteak quality.

name: Black Beauty
tagline: Darkest tomato ever bred, high anthocyanin
description: Among the darkest tomatoes in existence. Rich, smoky flavour. Looks dramatic on the plate.
regionNotes: Sun-loving. Greenhouse recommended in northern UK.

name: Early Girl
tagline: First ripe tomato of the season, every year
description: One of the earliest-maturing hybrids. First ripe fruit in just 57 days. Medium-sized red globes.
regionNotes: Top choice for UK, Scotland, Scandinavia, Canada. The reliable workhorse for cool regions.

name: Cuore di Bue
tagline: Italian oxheart, classic for caprese salad
description: Classic Italian heart-shaped oxheart variety. Large, meaty, low-seed fruit with concentrated sweet flavour.
regionNotes: Mediterranean climate native. UK growers need a sheltered warm spot or greenhouse.

name: Homestead
tagline: Heat-tolerant determinate, sets fruit in hot weather
description: Heat-tolerant determinate variety bred to set fruit reliably in hot conditions.
regionNotes: Best for warm and hot climates. Cool UK summers may underperform.

name: Sart Roloise
tagline: Rare Belgian heirloom, large ribbed fruit
description: Rare Belgian heirloom. Large, ribbed, deep-red beefsteaks with rich, complex flavour.
regionNotes: Adapted to northern European climates. Performs well in UK conditions.

name: Lemon Boy
tagline: Classic yellow slicer, mild and low-acid
description: Reliable hybrid yellow slicer with mild, low-acid flavour. Bright lemon-yellow skin.
regionNotes: Adaptable to most temperate climates including UK.

name: Sungold
tagline: Sweetest cherry tomato in the world
description: Often called the sweetest tomato ever bred. Golden-orange cherry tomatoes with extraordinarily sweet, fruity flavour.
regionNotes: Outstanding in every climate including UK and Scotland. The single best variety for new growers anywhere.

name: Mexico Midget
tagline: Tiny currant-sized tomatoes, intense flavour
description: Currant-sized tomatoes with intensely concentrated tomato flavour. Heirloom from Mexico.
regionNotes: Productive across temperate climates. Excellent in UK greenhouses.

name: Barry's Crazy Cherry
tagline: Massive trusses of 100+ pale yellow cherries
description: Famous for producing enormous trusses with over 100 small pale-yellow cherry tomatoes each.
regionNotes: Suited to temperate climates with a long warm season.

name: Super Sweet 100
tagline: Long trusses of bright red sweet cherries
description: Improved Sweet 100 with higher vitamin C content and even sweeter fruit.
regionNotes: Adaptable across temperate climates. Excellent UK performer.

name: Sun Sugar
tagline: Sungold's crack-resistant sibling
description: Similar sweetness to Sungold but with much better crack-resistance.
regionNotes: Excellent for variable climates. UK and northern Europe friendly.

name: Sweet Million
tagline: Crack-resistant alternative to Sweet 100
description: Improved disease and crack resistance over Sweet 100. Classic sweet tomato flavour.
regionNotes: Reliable across temperate climates including UK.

name: Juliet
tagline: Crack-proof grape tomato, AAS winner
description: All-America Selections winner. Mini-Roma shaped grape tomatoes that almost never crack.
regionNotes: Top performer across temperate climates. One of the most reliable for UK gardens.
